title: pastinaca lasiocarpa
intro: Pastinaca lasiocarpa, also known as wild parsnip, is a biennial herbaceous plant native to North America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pastinaca lasiocarpa include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and soothing digestive issues. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
